phrasal_verbs.meaning: start suddenly (for a war, fire, etc)
stop doing sth you do regularly
start to suffer from a minor illness
do less of (smoking, etc); reduce an amount of
stop being effective (for a drug, etc)

C.title: Original key word transformation practice
C.instructions: Complete the second sentence so that it keeps the same meaning as the first.

questions.sentence:
Mia began the class project before the lesson on Health and fitness. **DOWN**
The class project ...... before the lesson on Health and fitness.

Leo began the study group before the lesson on Health and fitness. **UP**
The study group ...... before the lesson on Health and fitness.

Sara began the exam workshop before the lesson on Health and fitness. **PUT**
The exam workshop ...... before the lesson on Health and fitness.
questions.answer: to put the dog down/to put down the dog

questions.sentence:
Tom began the online lesson before the lesson on Health and fitness. **UP**
The online lesson ...... before the lesson on Health and fitness.

Nina began the school club before the lesson on Health and fitness. **ON**
The school club ...... before the lesson on Health and fitness.

Oscar began the revision session before the lesson on Health and fitness. **OUT**
The revision session ...... before the lesson on Health and fitness.
